content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.1 WARN — description.txt claims "Built-in ad blocker", "AI-powered suggestions", and offline sync intelligence, but ContentView.swift:1-19 is only a plain in-memory task list with no Safari content blocker extension, ML/CoreML import, or persistence code.
Pierre: Apple rejects apps under 2.1/2.3.1 when marketing copy promises features reviewers cannot find in the binary, and an "ad blocker" claim requires a content blocker extension while "AI-powered suggestions" implies some ML or backend logic. I found no code evidence for any of these claims — no CoreML, no NetworkExtension/ContentBlocker target, and tasks aren't even persisted, undermining "works fully offline". Either implement these features before submission or rewrite the description to match the actual simple task-list functionality.
